clean_story: Final point.  Irreversible stop. "End" marks where something ceases—like a book’s last page, a candle’s flame, or a heartbeat’s final pulse. It’s the edge of a cliff, not a wall. A river doesn’t vanish at its end—it merges into an ocean. A story’s end lingers in memory. Even a star’s death scatters elements for new worlds. Ends create space. A breath held too long ends with relief. A chapter’s close starts another. Without ends, beginnings would drown in continuity. Three related subjects are cycles, legacy, and thresholds.
